adds.rationale: Replace failed single-name SMR (NuScale) with the Range Nuclear Renaissance ETF, which launched January 23 2024 and diversifies across uranium producers, nuclear utilities, advanced reactor developers, and construction services — maintaining the profile's nuclear-fission-for-AI-baseload thesis without the execution risk of one pre-revenue company whose only announced project just collapsed.

news_evidence.summary: Range Nuclear Renaissance ETF (NUKZ) begins trading on NYSE Arca on January 23, 2024, tracking the VettaFi Nuclear Renaissance index across four sub-segments: advanced reactors, utilities, construction and services, and fuel; provides diversified nuclear sector exposure vs. single-name risk.
NUKZ ETF overview confirms launch date of January 23, 2024 and index composition spanning uranium producers, nuclear utilities, SMR developers, and reactor services companies.

IAEA bulletin notes that data centres, AI workloads, and cryptocurrency operations are actively evaluating advanced nuclear power to meet growing always-on power needs — the structural driver behind nuclear-focused ETF demand in early 2024.

removes.rationale: Fundamental thesis breach: NuScale Power's only announced SMR project (the UAMPS Carbon Free Power Project in Idaho) was mutually terminated in November 2023 after per-kW costs more than doubled from initial estimates, leaving the company with no active deployment pipeline and zero near-term revenue path as of Q1 2024.

news_evidence.summary: UAMPS and NuScale Power mutually agree to terminate the Carbon Free Power Project; project could not advance due to insufficient subscriber commitments and costs that had risen from $9,964/kW in 2015 to $21,561/kW — higher than the actual cost of Vogtle Unit 3 and 4.

E&E News coverage of NuScale CFPP cancellation details the cost-surge dynamic: SMR economics did not scale as projected and the $1.4B DOE cost-share deal was insufficient to attract enough municipal utility subscribers at the revised price.

NuScale Power 8-K filed February 15, 2024 announces Q4 and full-year 2023 earnings call for March 14, 2024; as of the filing date the company has no active commercial project following the November 2023 CFPP termination.

rationale_overall: The single swap — SMR out, NUKZ in — is the only change warranted this quarter. NuScale Power lost its only announced SMR project to a cost collapse in November 2023; holding a pre-revenue single-name company with no active deployment path conflicts directly with the profile's 'enter during buildup, trim before the crest' rule when what has happened is not a crest but an outright execution failure. NUKZ, which launched on January 23 2024, replaces that exposure with a diversified nuclear-sector ETF (uranium producers, utilities, advanced reactor developers, construction services) that keeps the profile's nuclear-fission-for-AI-baseload thesis intact without concentrating on one company's broken pipeline. All other holdings are kept: NVDA is the dominant beneficiary of AI capex in Q1 2024 (revenue +262% YoY); RKLB closed Q1 with $1B+ backlog and 69% YoY revenue growth; IONQ reported 77% YoY revenue growth and extended its AWS access contract; GOOGL launched Gemini and beat Q1 revenue estimates; MSFT continues to monetize its OpenAI partnership through Azure; BOTZ maintains the profile's robotics-wave exposure; and ITA covers the defense/rearmament non-tech wave whose underlying holdings (GE Aerospace, ITT) reported strong Q1 results. The post-swap watchlist covers six distinct wave buckets (AI x3, nuclear, rockets/spacecraft, robotics, quantum, geopolitical/defense) within the eight-ticker cap.
